rxn_class_summary: Metal-catalyzed (Rh2 and Ru2 paddlewheel) intramolecular and intermolecular nitrogen-atom transfer / C–H amination of sulfamate, carbamate, sulfamide, and related N–H substrates using hypervalent iodine oxidants to give heterocycles or amines via metal nitrenoid intermediates.
electron_flow: PhI(OAc)2 condenses with sulfamate/carbamate N–H substrate to form an iminoiodinane (ROSO2N=IPh); coordination to the dirhodium (or diruthenium) paddlewheel extrudes PhI to give a metal nitrenoid, which transfers nitrogen to a C–H bond either by concerted asynchronous insertion (Rh2) or stepwise H-atom abstraction / radical rebound (Ru2(hp)4Cl).
rate_determining_step: For Rh2-catalyzed amination, formation of the iminoiodinane ROSO2N=IPh appears rate-limiting (no variance in rate across 0.5–5 mol% catalyst within first 100 s); the product-determining C–H insertion step itself is fast.
notes: Two competing mechanisms are discussed: (favored for Rh2) concerted asynchronous singlet nitrene C–H insertion supported by KIE ~2.6, Hammett ρ = -0.55, stereochemical retention at tertiary C–H, and failure of a 10^11 s^-1 cyclopropane radical clock to ring-open; (favored for [Ru2(hp)4Cl]) stepwise H-atom abstraction/radical rebound supported by larger KIE 4.9, Hammett ρ = -0.90, partial cis-trans alkene isomerization, cyclopropane clock ring-opening, and DFT. A one-electron / proton-coupled pathway via mixed-valent [Rh2(esp)2]+ (characterized by UV-vis, λ ~750 nm) is also proposed for intermolecular amination and can be driven by Ce(SO4)2 as a single-electron oxidant. Sulfilimine trapping with PhSMe provides indirect evidence for the transient iminoiodinane. Catalyst decomposition via ligand exchange between PhI(OAc)2 and Rh2(O2 13CMe)4 (release of 13C-labeled AcOH) is documented; chelating esp ligand suppresses this and stabilizes the mixed-valent state.
